06 May 2026 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

    Other Operations

    Operation Epic Fury / Operation Roaring Lion | Operation True Promise 4

  • US Warplane Fires On Iranian-Flagged Ship RFE/RL 06 May 2026 -- US military officials said a naval warplane fired on and disabled the rudder of an Iranian-flagged oil tanker that tried to break the US blockade of Iranian ports.
  • U.S. Forces Disable Vessel in Gulf of Oman Attempting to Violate Blockade CENTCOM 06 May 2026 -- U.S. forces operating in the Gulf of Oman enforced blockade measures by disabling an Iranian-flagged unladen oil tanker attempting to sail toward an Iranian port at 9 a.m. ET, May 6.

  • French Shipping Firm Says Vessel Attacked in Hormuz Strait RFE/RL 06 May 2026 -- French shipping group CMA CGM said one of its vessels was attacked while transiting the Strait of Hormuz, underscoring ongoing risks to commercial shipping amid the Iran conflict.

  • Iran's retaliatory attacks hit far more US military assets than acknowledged: Report Press TV 06 May 2026 -- Satellite imagery reveals Iran's retaliatory airstrikes inflicted significantly greater damage on US military assets across the region than acknowledged by the Trump administration.
  • May 6: Iran consolidates diplomatic, military leverage as Trump pulls plug on 'Project Freedom' Press TV 06 May 2026 -- In another humiliating retreat, US President Donald Trump announced the "pause" in so-called "Project Freedom" to force open the Strait of Hormuz after facing asymmetric Iranian deterrence.
  • IRGC says it will allow 'safe, stable' transit via Hormuz 'with aggressor threats neutralized' Press TV 06 May 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) has announced that transit through the Strait of Hormuz will gradually become safe and stable as the security situation in the Persian Gulf improves "with the aggressor's threats neutralized."

  • Trump Warns Of 'Much Higher Level' Strikes If Iran Refuses Deal RFE/RL 06 May 2026 -- US President Donald Trump warned Iran it would face "a much higher level and intensity" of strikes if it failed to agree to a peace deal that the two sides are reportedly closing in on.
  • War on Iran can end if Tehran agrees to terms: Trump Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 06 May 2026 -- US President Donald Trump on Wednesday said the US-Israeli war on Iran could come to an end if Tehran complies with the terms of a potential agreement, while warning that renewed bombing would follow if it refuses, Anadolu Agency reported.
  • Trump Vows to Halt Operation Epic Fury – But Only If Iran Bows to US Demands Sputnik 06 May 2026 -- The United States will end Operation Epic Fury only if Iran agrees to Washington's terms, President Donald Trump said on Wednesday.
  • Trump says U.S. military operation guiding ships out of Hormuz Strait to be paused People's Daily 06 May 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump said Tuesday that Project Freedom, the U.S. military operation aimed at guiding ships out of the Strait of Hormuz, will be paused to see whether a peace deal can be reached between Washington and Tehran.
  • Amid Iran's resilience, Trump says 'pausing' plan to reopen Strait of Hormuz Press TV 06 May 2026 -- US President Donald Trump says he is "pausing" his so-called "Project Freedom," a new interventionist military adventure with the self-described aim of reopening the Strait of Hormuz, amid strict controls being exercised over the waterway by Iran.
  • Trump skips ceasefire red line, claiming Iran wants deal People's Daily 06 May 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump on Tuesday refused to spell out what would constitute a violation of the fragile ceasefire with Iran, reiterating Tehran wants a peace deal with Washington.
  • Rubio says US combat operations against Iran are 'over'; Tehran warns threats continued even as diplomatic efforts were underway Global Times 06 May 2026 -- US Secretary of State Marco Rubio on Tuesday said that the combat operations launched in February against Iran had ended, while Tehran warned threats continued even as diplomatic efforts were underway.

  • Iran Responds To Talk Of Deal With US RFE/RL 06 May 2026 -- Following reports that an agreement between Washington and Tehran to end the war is imminent, a spokesman for Iran's Foreign Ministry said that Tehran is still considering the US proposal.
  • Iran Is Using Alternative Routes To Bypass US Naval Blockade RFE/RL 06 May 2026 -- The United States and Israel's bombing campaign has left much of Iran's infrastructure and industries in tatters, disrupting domestic production and hiking prices for basic food items.
  • Iran to confront any fresh attack with a strategy 'beyond enemy's calculations': IRGC official Press TV 06 May 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) Navy's deputy for political affairs warns adversaries about the unpredictable scale, shrewdness, and manner of Iran's response to any fresh aggression.
  • Iran ready for diplomacy to end war, but will uphold national rights, Pezeshkian tells Macron Press TV 06 May 2026 -- President Masoud Pezeshkian of Iran has told his French counterpart Emmanuel Macron that the Islamic Republic is ready to pursue in earnest diplomatic channels to end the war, but insists on securing the rights of the Iranian nation.
  • Iran rejects 'flawed' US resolution on Hormuz, says only viable solution is end of war Press TV 06 May 2026 -- Iran's permanent mission to the United Nations has reiterated that the only viable solution in the strategic Strait of Hormuz is a permanent end of hostilities against the Islamic Republic.
  • Iran's armed forces vow 'humiliating defeat' if US uses talks as deception tool Press TV 06 May 2026 -- Iran's armed forces remain strong and fully prepared to deliver a "humiliating defeat" to bullying adversaries if the United States uses potential negotiations with Tehran as a cover for further aggression, says a senior Iranian military official.
  • 'Negotiation does not mean coercion': Iran says it is reviewing US proposal Press TV 06 May 2026 -- Tehran says it is still reviewing Washington's latest proposal for a potential agreement to end the war, with no official response yet delivered.
  • Iranian, Saudi FMs call for regional cooperation to prevent tensions Press TV 06 May 2026 -- Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi and his Saudi counterpart Faisal bin Farhan Al Saud have underlined the need for cooperation among regional countries to prevent tension in the region.

  • Security Council holds closed-door talks following attacks on United Arab Emirates UN News 06 May 2026 -- As the crisis in the Strait of Hormuz continues, the United Nations reiterates its commitment to support efforts towards peace, UN Spokesperson Stéphane Dujarric said on Wednesday during his media briefing from New York.

  • Wang Yi Holds Talks with Iranian Foreign Minister Seyyed Abbas Araghchi PRC MofA 06 May 2026 -- On May 6, 2026, Member of the Political Bureau of the CPC Central Committee and Foreign Minister Wang Yi held talks with Iranian Foreign Minister Seyyed Abbas Araghchi in Beijing. 
  • Chinese FM meets Iranian counterpart amid tension in Mideast, stressing China ready to help restart talks, de-escalate Mideast crisis Global Times 06 May 2026 -- Amid lingering tensions in the Strait of Hormuz and with no peace agreement between Tehran and Washington finalized yet, Iranian Foreign Minister Seyyed Abbas Araghchi has arrived in Beijing on Wednesday, as the Chinese side stressed that China will remain committed to deescalating tensions and ending hostilities, and continue to provide support for initiating peace talks, playing a greater role in restoring peace and tranquility in the Middle East.
  • Chinese FM responds to Bessent's claim of calling on Beijing's support for US operation in Hormuz Global Times 06 May 2026 -- Asked to comment on recent claims by US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, who claimed that he has called on China to join an American operation to escort ships through the Strait of Hormuz and alleged that China blocks initiatives at the United Nations, given Washington's long-standing practice of conducting unilateral military interventions and bypassing the UN, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Lin Jian said on Wednesday that China's position on the situation in the strait is clear-cut.
  • China believes post-war Iran has higher international standing: Araghchi Press TV 06 May 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi held a "constructive meeting" with his Chinese counterpart Wang Yi in Beijing on Wednesday, saying China shares Tehran's view that the US-Israeli war has fundamentally changed Iran's position in the region and internationally.
  • China blasts 'illegitimate' war on Iran as it hosts FM Araghchi Press TV 06 May 2026 -- China has hosted Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi for talks in Beijing, with Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi calling the US-Israeli war on Tehran "illegitimate" and urging a complete ceasefire.
  • Wang Yi, Araghchi Urge Negotiations and Regional Stability Sputnik 06 May 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi and his Chinese counterpart Wang Yi have held talks in Beijing.
  • Beijing Urges Immediate Cease-Fire In Iran As Trump-Xi Summit Nears RFE/RL 06 May 2026 -- Beijing has reiterated its call for a "comprehensive cease-fire" in the Iran-US conflict and for a return to normal passage through the Strait of Hormuz as Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi kicked off his first visit to China since the conflict erupted at the end of February.

  • HE GCCSG: The GCC Categorically Rejects Iran's False Allegations Against the UAE and Reaffirms Full Support in the Face of Such Claims and Violations Gulf Cooperation Council 06 May 2026
  • Macron Calls For End To Hormuz Blockade RFE/RL 06 May 2026 -- French President Emmanuel Macron called for restoring ship traffic to the Strait of Hormuz, and he condemned Iran's "unjustified" strikes against civilian ships and targets in the United Arab Emirates.

  • Ex-NATO Commander Breedlove: Iran Crisis Exposes A West 'More Divided Than Its Adversaries' RFE/RL 06 May 2026 -- As tensions rise in the Strait of Hormuz and Washington pushes allies to step up, the debate is no longer only about whether NATO has the capabilities to contribute -- but whether the alliance can manage another major security test without deepening transatlantic friction.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Site Selections Announced for Directed-Energy Counter-Drone Program DoD News 06 May 2026 -- On behalf of the War Department, Joint Interagency Task Force 401 selected five installations to participate in the directed-energy counter-unmanned aircraft systems pilot program included in the Fiscal Year 2026 National Defense Authorization Act.
  • Utah National Guard Integrates Air, Ground Capabilities During Exercise Wolverine DoD News 06 May 2026 -- The sounds of saw blades against concrete, the striking of hammers against nails and the movement of debris amid collapsed rubble echoed across the western training area at Camp Williams, Utah, during an Exercise Wolverine simulated search and extraction scenario, April 30.
  • F-22 Raptors arrive in Japan, strengthen Indo-Pacific airpower US Air Force 06 May 2026 -- U.S. Air Force F-22 Raptors assigned to the 90th Fighter Squadron, Joint Base Elmendorf-Richardson, Alaska, and the 27th Fighter Squadron, Joint Base Langley-Eustis, Virginia, arrived at Kadena Air Base, marking the latest fighter rotation supporting operations across the Indo-Pacific region.
  • ANG leaders convene to align on wartime readiness, 'bring the future faster' US Air Force 06 May 2026 -- Maj. Gen. Duke Pirak, Air National Guard acting director, and Chief Master Sgt. Josh Moore, ANG command chief, hosted ANG wing commanders and senior enlisted leaders from across the nation to discuss readiness and how to bring the future faster during the annual Wing Leader Fly-In at McGhee Tyson Air National Guard Base, April 29-30.
  • U.S. Coast Guard creates new Special Missions Command to counter maritime threats at home, abroad US Coast Guard 06 May 2026 -- The Coast Guard is standing up the Special Missions Command to oversee its deployable specialized forces. The command will enhance the operational effectiveness of the Coast Guard in responding to a wide range of national emergencies and events as the demand for deployable specialized forces capabilities increase.
  • B-52 Engine Replacement Program Holds Critical Design Review, Paves Way for B-52J Upgrade USSTRATCOM 06 May 2026 -- The B-52J Commercial Engine Replacement Program (CERP) recently held its Critical Design Review (CDR), a pivotal milestone that enables the modification of two B-52 aircraft with new engines.
  • Balikatan 2026: MRF-D 26, joint, allied forces simulate maritime security on Balabac Island USMC News 06 May 2026 -- U.S. Marines with Marine Rotational Force - Darwin 26, a now-certified Special-Purpose Marine Air-Ground Task Force assigned to rotational deployments in the South Pacific, participated in a live-fire assault to secure and hold Balabac Airfield in a demonstration of multi-domain interoperability alongside U.S. Soldiers from 25th Infantry Division, Philippine marines and Australian soldiers during Exercise Balikatan 2026, May 3.

  • NSPA and NCIA reaffirm strategic partnership to improve delivery of Alliance capabilities NATO Support and Procurement Agency (NSPA) 06 May 2026 -- On 5 May, the NATO Support and Procurement Agency (NSPA) and the NATO Communications and Information Agency (NCIA) convened at a partnership board at NSPA Headquarters in Luxembourg, reaffirming their enduring cooperation in support of NATO's operational readiness.
  • NATO and Bosnia and Herzegovina discuss strengthening cooperation NATO 06 May 2026 -- On Wednesday (6 May 2026), NATO's Deputy Secretary General Radmila Shekerinska met with the Presidency of Bosnia and Herzegovina, who also participated in a meeting of the North Atlantic Council. Ms Shekerinska underlined NATO's unwavering commitment to Bosnia and Herzegovina, and the importance that lasting peace and security in the country and stability across the Western Balkans have for security in the Euro-Atlantic area. She welcomed the useful exchange of views, and the continuation of a robust high-level dialogue between NATO and Bosnia and Herzegovina.

  • Units participating in the Drone Line initiative will be able to order UAVs through the DOT-Chain Defence weapons marketplace Ukraine MOD 06 May 2026 -- Units participating in the Drone Line initiative can now order UAVs and other equipment through the DOT-Chain Defence weapons marketplace.
  • More than UAH 3.2 million in assistance provided to Ukrainians affected by russian mines Ukraine MOD 06 May 2026 -- Through the database of the National Mine Action Agency, 180 people affected by mine incidents have already received assistance worth more than UAH 3.2 million.

  • Submarines conduct twin visits in Port Phillip Australia DOD 06 May 2026 -- Two Collins-class submarines have slipped between the Bellarine and Mornington peninsulas into Port Phillip for concurrent port visits in Melbourne and Geelong.

  • Raksha Mantri holds bilateral talks with Deputy Prime Minister & Minister of National Defence of Vietnam in New Delhi India PIB 06 May 2026 -- Raksha Mantri Shri Rajnath Singh held a bilateral meeting with Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of National Defence of Vietnam General Phan Van Giang in New Delhi on May 06, 2026. The meeting was marked by warmth and growing strategic trust, reflecting the deep-rooted Enhanced Comprehensive Strategic Partnership between India and Vietnam.
  • India-Algeria Joint Commission agrees to further expand defence cooperation during inaugural meeting in New Delhi India PIB 06 May 2026 -- The inaugural Joint Commission meeting between India and Algeria took place in New Delhi on May 05, 2026. During the meeting, the two sides discussed areas of mutual interest in the field of training, military exercise, medical cooperation and defence industries. They agreed to further expand defence cooperation activities between the two countries.
  • VISIT OF DEPUTY COMMANDER ROYAL NETHERLANDS NAVY AND HNLMS DE RUYTER TO KOCHI India PIB 06 May 2026 -- Royal Netherlands Navy Ship HNLMS De Ruyter (F804), a De Zeven Provinciën-class frigate, arrived at Kochi on 04 May 2026.

  • APP: Marka-e-Haq – Where Pakistan's air warriors redefined aerial combat & became undisputed 'King of Skies' Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 06 May 2026 -- The Marka-e-Haq 2025 engagement has emerged as a defining case study in modern air combat, where the Pakistan Air Force (PAF) exhibited precision strike capability, multi-domain integration, and cyber operations to offset the numerical advantage of the Indian Air Force (IAF), Associated Press of Pakistan (APP) reported.

  • China responds to Japan launching offensive missiles overseas Global Times 06 May 2026 -- A Chinese foreign ministry spokesperson said on Wednesday Japan's right-wing forces are pushing for the acceleration of remilitarization, adding Japan's neo-militarism is rising and posing threat to regional peace and stability.

  • Joint Statement - Nordic Defence Cooperation Government of Norway 06 May 2026 -- On May 5-6 the Nordic Ministers of Defence gathered in Trøndelag under Norway's chairship of NORDEFCO in 2026.

  • The Lithuanian and Polish Presidents observed joint military exercise in the Suwałki Corridor territory President of the Republic of Lithuania 06 May 2026 -- On Wednesday, President Gitanas Nausėda, together with Polish President Karol Nawrocki, observed the international tactical exercise Brave Griffon 2026, involving Lithuanian and Polish troops.

  • Japanese missile system 'sinks' PH target vessel in Balikatan drills Philippine News Agency 06 May 2026 -- The Japanese Type 88 surface-to-ship missile system, fired for the first time in a Balikatan exercise, sank the decommissioned patrol corvette, former BRP Quezon (PS-70), during the maritime strike (MARSTRIKE) drills conducted off Ilocos Norte Wednesday.
  • PH can now buy combat equipment from Japan Philippine News Agency 06 May 2026 -- Department of National Defense (DND) Secretary Gilberto Teodoro Jr. on Tuesday night said the Philippines can now buy military weapons from the Japanese aside from acquiring these through transfers, as Tokyo eases up its restrictions on the transfer of its defense equipment and technology.

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

  • 6 May 2026 - Daily Press Briefing by the Office of the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General United Nations 06 May 2026

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots | Roaring Lion (Lebanon)

  • Joint Statement from PM Netanyahu and Defense Minister Israel Katz Israel - Prime Minister 06 May 2026 -- "Pursuant to the approval of Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Defense Minister Israel Katz, the IDF has targeted the commander of the Hezbollah terrorist organization's Radwan Force in Beirut in order to eliminate him."

  • Israel Targets Hezbollah Unit Commander In Beirut Suburb Amid Cease-Fire RFE/RL 06 May 2026 -- Israeli forces struck a Beirut suburb on May 6 for the first time since a cease-fire took effect three weeks ago, targeting and reportedly killing a commander of Hezbollah's Radwan special forces unit.
  • Lebanon's PM: Negotiations with Israel do not mean normalization Press TV 06 May 2026 -- Lebanon's Prime Minister Salam Nawaf says representatives from the Beirut government are engaged in negotiations with Israeli counterparts, but the Arab nation is not seeking normalization with the occupying regime.
  • Israeli attacks on Lebanon kill 7 more people, including paramedics Press TV 06 May 2026 -- Israel has carried out new drone and airstrikes across southern and eastern Lebanon, leaving at least seven people killed and six others wounded, including paramedics, despite an ongoing ceasefire.

  • Gaza flotilla: Aid delivery 'not a crime', UN rights office tells Israel UN News 06 May 2026 -- The UN human rights office on Wednesday called on Israel to immediately release two members of the Global Sumud Flotilla detained in international waters, warning that solidarity with Palestinians and attempts to deliver humanitarian aid to Gaza "is not a crime."
  • Israel must release Gaza-bound flotilla activists OHCHR 06 May 2026 -- "Israel must immediately and unconditionally release Global Sumud Flotilla members Saif Abukeshek and Thiago de Avila, who were detained in international waters and brought to Israel where they continue to be held without charge."
  • UNRWA Situation Report #9 on the Lebanon Emergency Response 2026 (29 April 2026) UNRWA 06 May 2026
  • UNRWA Situation Report #219 on the Humanitarian Crisis in the Occupied Palestinian Territory (the Gaza Strip and the West Bank, including East Jerusalem) UNRWA 06 May 2026

  • The European Union, France, Germany and Palestine Launch the TAJDID Project to Strengthen Water, Sanitation and Agricultural Reuse in Jenin West European External Action Service (EEAS) 06 May 2026 -- The Palestinian Ministry of Finance and Planning (MoFP), together with the Palestinian Water Authority (PWA), the European Union (EU), the Agence Française de Développement (AFD), the German Development Bank (KfW), and the Jenin West Water Utility (JWWU), signed yesterday two key agreements to launch a major wastewater treatment and reuse project in Jenin West.
  • Bundeswehr mission for UNIFIL concludes Germany - Federal Government 06 May 2026 -- Cabinet decision

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Lebanon, Occupied Palestinian Territory, Chad, Ukraine UNOCHA 06 May 2026 -- Civilian death and injuries continue despite ceasefire

  • 62 Chinese naval, coast guard craft sighted in WPS in April Philippine News Agency 06 May 2026 -- A total of 62 Chinese naval and coast guard ships were spotted in the four key features of the West Philippine Sea (WPS) in April, significantly lower than the 90 recorded last March.
  • PH upbeat on South China Sea code of conduct as talks intensify Philippine News Agency 06 May 2026 -- The Philippines remains cautiously optimistic over progress in negotiations for a long-awaited Code of Conduct (COC) in the South China Sea, even as energy security and cooperation are expected to take center stage in the country's hosting of the 2026 Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) Summit in Cebu this week.

  • OIC Condemns the Terrorist Attack in Chad Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 06 May 2026 -- The General Secretariat of the Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) has condemned in the strongest terms the terrorist attack that targeted a defense and security forces base in the Lake Chad region on 4 May 2026, which resulted in the deaths and injuries of dozens of soldiers

News Reports

  • Meeting with Head of Tambov Region Yevgeny Pervyshov President of Russia 06 May 2026 -- Vladimir Putin held a working meeting with Head of the Tambov Region Yevgeny Pervyshov.
  • Statement by the Head of the Delegation of the Russian Federation, Ambassador-at-Large Andrey Belousov at the 11th Review Conference of the Parties to the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons (Main Committee I, Nuclear Disarmament), New York, 5 May 2026 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 06 May 2026
  • Statement by the Deputy Head of the Delegation of the Russian Federation at the 11th Review Conference of the Parties to the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons, Deputy Director of the Department for Non-Proliferation and Arms Control of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Russian Federation M.Kondratenkov (Cluster II, Non-Proliferation and IAEA Safeguards), New York, 5 May 2026 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 06 May 2026

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Lin Jian's Regular Press Conference on May 6, 2026 PRC MofA 06 May 2026
  • "You may stay tuned": Chinese FM responds to query on timing and details regarding Trump's reported China visit Global Times 06 May 2026 -- Responding to a media inquiry on whether the Chinese side could provide specific timing and details regarding US President Donald Trump's visit to China, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Lin Jian said, "You may stay tuned."
  • Wang Yi Holds Talks with Bangladeshi Foreign Minister Khalilur Rahman PRC MofA 06 May 2026 -- On May 6, 2026, Member of the Political Bureau of the CPC Central Committee and Foreign Minister Wang Yi held talks in Beijing with Bangladeshi Foreign Minister Khalilur Rahman during the latter's visit to China.

  • Diehard 'Taiwan independence' separatists and their family members will never be allowed to profit from investment, business in mainland: Taiwan Affairs Office Global Times 06 May 2026 -- "We will never allow diehard 'Taiwan independence' separatists and their family members to profit from investment or business activities in the mainland. Nor will we allow those who support 'Taiwan independence' and undermine cross-Straits relations to make money in the mainland while backing separatist activities," Chen Binhua, a spokesperson for the Taiwan Affairs Office of the State Council, said on Wednesday.
  • Lai's sneaky Eswatini visit slammed as a scandalous stunt Global Times 06 May 2026 -- Following his "stowaway-like" travel to Eswatini, Taiwan regional leader Lai Ching-te once again sneaked aboard a flight and forced his way through relevant countries' skies to return to Taiwan island on Tuesday. His clandestine trip has continued to draw criticism from the island and the mainland.
  • U.S., China both believe a stable Taiwan Strait beneficial: Rubio CNA 06 May 2026 -- U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Tuesday the United States and China both see maintaining stability in the Taiwan Strait as in their interests as the leaders of the two countries prepare to meet next week, with Taiwan likely on the agenda.
  • Ministry of National Defense Issues Press Release Regarding "PLA Joint Combat Readiness Patrol" ROC MND 06 May 2026
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 06 May 2026

  • Two U.S. Nationals Sentenced for Facilitating Fraudulent Remote Information Technology Worker Schemes to Generate Revenue for the Democratic People's Republic of Korea US Dept. of Justice 06 May 2026 -- These Represent the 7th and 8th Sentences of U.S.-Based "Laptop Farmers" Secured in last 5 Months as part of Ongoing Efforts to Disrupt North Korea's Illicit Revenue Generation
  • Seoul at UN reaffirms 'phased, practical' method toward NK nukes KOREA.net 06 May 2026 -- The government has reaffirmed its phased and practical approach to resolve North Korea's nuclear issue.

  • PM Netanyahu's Remarks at the Start of the Security Cabinet Meeting Israel - Prime Minister 06 May 2026 -- "We are maintaining continuous contact with our friends in the US. I speak with President Trump on an almost daily basis."

  • Philippine Statement on the Transfer of Daw Aung San Suu Kyi to House Arrest Philippines DFA 06 May 2026 -- The Department of Foreign Affairs welcomes reports regarding the transfer of Daw Aung San Suu Kyi to house arrest and the partial reduction of her sentence, alongside the broad amnesty granted to over 1,500 political prisoners in observance of the Full Moon Day of Kason.
  • ASEAN chair asks Myanmar for 'brief access' to Aung San Suu Kyi Philippine News Agency 06 May 2026 -- The Philippines, as chair of the 2026 Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN), has requested Myanmar to grant its special envoy "brief access" to Aung San Suu Kyi following her reported transfer to house arrest.

  • Transcript of Special Briefing by MEA on the State Visit of General Secretary, President of Vietnam to India India MEA 06 May 2026

  • Press conference - Sydney Prime Minister of Australia 06 May 2026
